128	/*
129	 * Compute the largest possible bucketed window given the time type and
130	 * internal restrictions.
131	 *
132	 * The largest bucketed window is governed by restrictions set by the type and
133	 * internal, TimescaleDB-specific legacy details (see get_max_window above for
134	 * further explanation).
135	 */
136	static InternalTimeRange
137	get_largest_bucketed_window(Oid timetype, int64 bucket_width)
138	{
139		InternalTimeRange maxwindow = {
140			.type = timetype,
141			.start = ts_time_get_min(timetype),
142			.end = ts_time_get_end_or_max(timetype),
143		};
144		InternalTimeRange maxbuckets = {
145			.type = timetype,
146		};
147	
148		/* For the MIN value, the corresponding bucket either falls on the exact
149		 * MIN or it will be below it. Therefore, we add (bucket_width - 1) to
150		 * move to the next bucket to be within the allowed range. */
151		maxwindow.start = ts_time_saturating_add(maxwindow.start, bucket_width - 1, timetype);
152		maxbuckets.start = ts_time_bucket_by_type(bucket_width, maxwindow.start, timetype);
153		maxbuckets.end = ts_time_get_end_or_max(timetype);
154	
155		return maxbuckets;
156	}
157	
158	/*
159	 * Adjust the refresh window to align with inscribed buckets, so it includes buckets, which are
160	 * fully covered by the refresh window.
161	 *
162	 * Bucketing refresh window is necessary for a continuous aggregate refresh, which can refresh only
163	 * entire buckets. The result of the function is a bucketed window, where its start is at the start
164	 * of the first bucket, which is  fully inside the refresh window, and its end is at the end of the
165	 * last fully covered bucket.
166	 *
167	 * Example1, the window needs to shrink:
168	 *    [---------)      - given refresh window
169	 * .|....|....|....|.  - buckets
170	 *       [----)        - inscribed bucketed window
171	 *
172	 * Example2, the window is already aligned:
173	 *       [----)        - given refresh window
174	 * .|....|....|....|.  - buckets
175	 *       [----)        - inscribed bucketed window
176	 *
177	 * This function is called for the continuous aggregate policy and manual refresh. In such case
178	 * excluding buckets, which are not fully covered by the refresh window, avoids refreshing a bucket,
179	 * where part of its data were dropped by a retention policy. See #2198 for details.
180	 */
181	static InternalTimeRange
182	compute_inscribed_bucketed_refresh_window(const ContinuousAgg *cagg,
183											  const InternalTimeRange *const refresh_window,
184											  const int64 bucket_width)
185	{
186		Assert(cagg != NULL);
187		Assert(cagg->bucket_function != NULL);
188	
189		NullableDatum NULL_DATUM = INIT_NULL_DATUM;
190		InternalTimeRange result = *refresh_window;
191		InternalTimeRange largest_bucketed_window =
192			get_largest_bucketed_window(refresh_window->type, bucket_width);
193	
194		if (refresh_window->start <= largest_bucketed_window.start)
195		{
196			result.start = largest_bucketed_window.start;
197		}
198		else
199		{
200			/* The start time needs to be aligned with the first fully enclosed bucket.
201			 * So the original window start is moved to next bucket, except if the start is
202			 * already aligned with a bucket, thus 1 is subtracted to avoid moving into next
203			 * bucket in the aligned case. */
204			int64 included_bucket =
205				ts_time_saturating_add(refresh_window->start, bucket_width - 1, refresh_window->type);
206			/* Get the start of the included bucket. */
207			result.start = ts_time_bucket_by_type_extended(bucket_width,
208														   included_bucket,
209														   refresh_window->type,
210														   NULL_DATUM,
211														   NULL_DATUM);
212		}
213	
214		if (refresh_window->end >= largest_bucketed_window.end)
215		{
216			result.end = largest_bucketed_window.end;
217		}
218		else
219		{
220			/* The window is reduced to the beginning of the bucket, which contains the exclusive
221			 * end of the refresh window. */
222			result.end = ts_time_bucket_by_type_extended(bucket_width,
223														 refresh_window->end,
224														 refresh_window->type,
225														 NULL_DATUM,
226														 NULL_DATUM);
227		}
228		return result;
229	}

-- Make sure that timezone can't be used for types other that timestamptz

CREATE TABLE conditions(
  day timestamp NOT NULL, -- not timestamptz!
  city text NOT NULL,
  temperature INT NOT NULL);

SELECT create_hypertable(
  'conditions', 'day',
  chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '1 day'
);

\set ON_ERROR_STOP 0
-- timebucket_ng is deprecated and can not be used in new CAggs anymore.
-- However, using this GUC the restriction can be lifted in debug builds
-- to ensure the functionality can be tested.
SET timescaledb.debug_allow_cagg_with_deprecated_funcs = true;

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW conditions
WITH (timescaledb.continuous, timescaledb.materialized_only=true) AS
SELECT city,
   timescaledb_experimental.time_bucket_ng('1 month', day, 'Europe/Moscow') AS bucket,
   MIN(temperature),
   MAX(temperature)
FROM conditions
GROUP BY city, bucket
WITH NO DATA;

-- Reset GUC to check if the CAgg would also work in release builds
RESET timescaledb.debug_allow_cagg_with_deprecated_funcs;

\set ON_ERROR_STOP 1

DROP TABLE conditions CASCADE;

CREATE TABLE conditions_tz(
  day timestamptz NOT NULL,
  city text NOT NULL,
  temperature INT NOT NULL);

SELECT create_hypertable(
  'conditions_tz', 'day',
  chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '1 day'
);

INSERT INTO conditions_tz (day, city, temperature) VALUES
  ('2021-06-14 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 26),
  ('2021-06-15 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 22),
  ('2021-06-16 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 24),

  ('2021-06-19 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 28),
  ('2021-06-20 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 30),
  ('2021-06-21 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 31),
  ('2021-06-22 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 34),
  ('2021-06-23 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 34),
  ('2021-06-24 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 34),
  ('2021-06-25 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 32),
  ('2021-06-26 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 32),
  ('2021-06-27 00:00:00 MSK', 'Moscow', 31);

\set ON_ERROR_STOP 0

-- Check that the name of the timezone is validated
SET timescaledb.debug_allow_cagg_with_deprecated_funcs = true;

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW conditions_summary_tz
WITH (timescaledb.continuous, timescaledb.materialized_only=true) AS
SELECT city,
   timescaledb_experimental.time_bucket_ng('1 month', day, 'Europe/Ololondon') AS bucket,
   MIN(temperature),
   MAX(temperature)
FROM conditions_tz
GROUP BY city, bucket
WITH NO DATA;

-- Check that buckets like '1 month 15 days' (fixed-sized + variable-sized) are not allowed

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW conditions_summary_tz
WITH (timescaledb.continuous, timescaledb.materialized_only=true) AS
SELECT city,
   timescaledb_experimental.time_bucket_ng('1 month 15 days', day, 'MSK') AS bucket,
   MIN(temperature),
   MAX(temperature)
FROM conditions_tz
GROUP BY city, bucket
WITH NO DATA;

-- Check that only immutable expressions can be used as a timezone

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW conditions_summary_tz
WITH (timescaledb.continuous, timescaledb.materialized_only=true) AS
SELECT city,
   timescaledb_experimental.time_bucket_ng('1 day', day, city) AS bucket,
   MIN(temperature),
   MAX(temperature)
FROM conditions_tz
GROUP BY city, bucket
WITH NO DATA;

\set ON_ERROR_STOP 1

-- Make sure it's possible to create an empty cagg (WITH NO DATA) and
-- that all the information about the bucketing function will be saved
-- to the TS catalog.

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW conditions_summary_tz
WITH (timescaledb.continuous, timescaledb.materialized_only=true) AS
SELECT city,
   timescaledb_experimental.time_bucket_ng('1 month', day, 'MSK') AS bucket,
   MIN(temperature),
   MAX(temperature)
FROM conditions_tz

content.text: I found the likely failure path: NULL `end_offset` produces a maximal timestamp refresh end, and variable bucket alignment for timezone buckets calls `time_bucket(..., timezone)` on that near-maximum timestamp, which can overflow during timezone conversion. Next I’m checking constants/usages so the boundary fix matches existing semantics.

SET ROLE :ROLE_DEFAULT_PERM_USER;

  sensor_id integer NOT NULL,
  value double precision NOT NULL,
  ts timestamptz NOT NULL
);
SELECT create_hypertable('metrics_tz_hourly', 'ts', chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '1 day');

INSERT INTO metrics_tz_hourly
SELECT 1, 1.0, t
FROM generate_series('2024-01-01 00:00+00'::timestamptz,
                     '2024-01-03 00:00+00'::timestamptz,
                     INTERVAL '1 hour') AS t;

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW metrics_tz_hourly_cagg
WITH (timescaledb.continuous, timescaledb.materialized_only = true) AS
SELECT time_bucket(INTERVAL '1 hour', ts, 'Asia/Dubai') AS bucket,
       sensor_id,
       count(*) AS count_items
FROM metrics_tz_hourly
GROUP BY bucket, sensor_id
WITH NO DATA;

SELECT add_continuous_aggregate_policy('metrics_tz_hourly_cagg',
  start_offset => INTERVAL '3 months',
  end_offset => NULL,
  schedule_interval => INTERVAL '1 hour') AS job_id \gset

INSERT INTO metrics_tz_hourly
SELECT 1, 1.0, t
FROM generate_series('2024-01-03 01:00+00'::timestamptz,
                     '2024-01-04 00:00+00'::timestamptz,
                     INTERVAL '1 hour') AS t;

CALL run_job(:job_id);

SELECT count(*) > 0 AS refreshed_rows,
       min(bucket) IS NOT NULL AS has_min_bucket,
       max(bucket) IS NOT NULL AS has_max_bucket
FROM metrics_tz_hourly_cagg;

DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW metrics_tz_hourly_cagg;
DROP TABLE metrics_tz_hourly;
""".lstrip()

content.text:
Implemented the fix without committing changes.

Summary:
- Located the continuous aggregate refresh-window bucketing path for variable-sized buckets in:
  - `src/ts_catalog/continuous_agg.c`
  - `tsl/src/continuous_aggs/refresh.c`
  - `tsl/src/bgw_policy/job.c`
- Identified the failure mode: policies with `end_offset => NULL` use the maximum/end timestamp as the refresh end. For timezone-based `time_bucket`, the variable-bucket refresh code tried to run that end timestamp through timezone-aware bucketing, which can overflow and raise `timestamp out of range`.
- Fixed `ts_compute_inscribed_bucketed_refresh_window_variable()` to avoid applying timezone bucket alignment to the exclusive maximum/end timestamp. It now preserves that end boundary directly instead of passing it through `time_bucket`.

Changed file:
- `src/ts_catalog/continuous_agg.c`

Validation:
- Created a temporary regression SQL test reproducing an hourly `time_bucket(..., 'Asia/Dubai')` continuous aggregate policy with `end_offset => NULL`.
- Removed the temporary test files afterward as requested.
- Built successfully with:
  - `make -j2`
- Attempted to run the temporary test before and after the fix with:
  - `make installcheck-t cagg_tz_hourly_policy_tmp`
- Test execution could not proceed in this environment because PostgreSQL `initdb` refuses to run as root:
  - `initdb: error: cannot be run as root`

No commits were made.
